reproducible_artifact: Deprecation-review checklist with the stranger-test predicate + reassignment rule
embodiment_sketch: Tech-debt / deprecation-review checklist line: 'For any deprecation blocked > 1 quarter, ask: would the assigned engineer be blocked if a stranger had built the system? If NO, the loyalty is misplaced; reassign the deprecation to an engineer without the loyalty entanglement and open a 1:1 with the original engineer about the orbit pattern. If YES (the engineer can list 3 specific invariants the legacy preserves that the replacement does not), the loyalty is right; the deprecation needs architectural review, not reassignment.'
verdict_reason: The verse's claim is encoded in a literal checklist line with a clean predicate (the stranger-test) and a clear branching action (reassign vs. architectural review). Tags include deprecation-management — structural. A senior engineer / EM could add this to their team's actual practice on Monday. The artifact concretely embodies the verse's diagnostic move (distinguishing right-loyalty from misplaced-loyalty). PASS.
